title: May I have…
pedagogy: Vocabulary: ขอ (high ข + live ออ → rising), ข้าว (high ข + mai tho → falling), น้ำ (LOW น + mai tho → high; written short ◌ำ, spoken long). Pattern: ขอ + thing + particle is the polite order — Thai politeness lives in ขอ and the particle, not a separate 'please'.

exercises.title: ขอ is the only “please” you need
body: Start a request with ขอ — “may I have” — and end with ครับ/ค่ะ. That sandwich is fully polite; Thai doesn't need an extra “please” in the middle.
ขอข้าวครับ gets you rice, ขอน้ำค่ะ gets you water. Swap the middle word, keep the sandwich.
You already know this ขอ: it's the first syllable of ขอโทษ — literally “I ask for the blame”.

exercises.explanation: ขอ + thing + particle. Note the tones: ขอ rises, ข้าว falls — same letter ข, different marks.
Hear น้ำ's high tone? That's mai tho on a low-class น — the rule you'll meet again and again.
